The recv call writes network data into the buffer starting at data+dataLen with size 100-dataLen-1. If dataLen >= 100, the size underflows to a large value, causing a buffer overflow. Even with smaller dataLen, if the buffer is not at least 100 bytes, recv writes out-of-bounds. This can lead to memory corruption and potential code execution.
